shadows: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.03) 0px 0px 0px 1px inset, rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.04) 0px 1px 0px 0px inset, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6) 0px 0px 0px 1px, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1) 0px 4px 4px 0px

taste.name: Restraint of Brand Accent
taste.trigger: When selecting background and interactive colors for the workspace tool
taste.decision: Chose an entirely near-black background (#08090a) and white/gray typography (#f7f8f8, #d0d6e0), restricting primary brand accents (#5e6ad2) strictly to key interactive surfaces
taste.reason: Because on near-black backgrounds, high contrast white text already draws strong visual attention. Adding excessive bright branding colors would distract the user from content and cheapen the aesthetic to a generic template
evidence: accentCandidates are primarily whites and grays, with bright purple used only for primary button actions

taste.name: Engraved Depth over Projected Layers
taste.trigger: When defining bounds and visual weight for layout panels and card surfaces
taste.decision: Chose thin inset borders and multi-layered border outlines (1px border-color #23252a inset) instead of standard fuzzy outer drop shadows
taste.reason: Because on dark canvases, drop shadows look muddy and imply separated floating sections. Crisp inset borders make panels feel engraved into the interface, reflecting the precision of a professional developer tool

taste.name: Macro-pauses via Massive Section Gaps
taste.trigger: When spacing out content modules along the vertical scroll layout
taste.decision: Chose massive, deliberate vertical gaps of 224px to 274px over standard 80px–100px block margins
taste.reason: Because massive vertical margins create natural pauses that isolate distinct features, ensuring the user digests one key capability before moving to the next, prioritising focus over a compact scroll footprint

taste.name: Optical Typographic Weights
taste.trigger: When setting typographic weights for display headers and large titles
taste.decision: Chose custom intermediate optical weights (510 weight for H1/H2 sizes, 590 weight for subheadings) instead of standard bold (700) or medium (500)
taste.reason: Because at large display sizes (48px–72px), standard bold weights bloat letter forms and clog white space, while custom intermediate weights retain high legibility and precise visual balance
